abstract: ABSTRACT Growing numbers of consumers are turning to Generative AI (GenAI) for mental health support, yet the psychological engagement processes underlying consumer–GenAI therapy interactions remain insufficiently understood. This gap limits our ability to explain why consumer experiences diverge, particularly in emotionally sensitive contexts requiring high levels of engagement. To address that, we examine why these experiences vary and what mechanisms contribute to these conflicting outcomes. Drawing on 30 semi‐structured interviews, we develop an integrative framework outlining an engagement process between consumers and GenAI therapy. Grounded in cognitive appraisal theory, the framework shows that GenAI operates simultaneously as a functional service and a relational agent, meaning that consumer decisions are shaped by both utilitarian and relational evaluations. By explaining how consumers appraise, reappraise, and cope with GenAI therapy, the framework clarifies the mechanisms underlying divergent outcomes and offers insights for identifying potential risks in GenAI mental health services.

ur.ai_reason: 核心即GenAI心理治疗访谈研究，含用户访谈方法
ur.tldr: 研究发现，消费者在使用生成式AI进行心理治疗时体验差异显著。基于30次半结构化访谈，框架揭示AI兼具功能服务与关系代理双重角色，消费者的功利评估与关系评估共同驱动决策。认知评价过程解释体验分歧，并提示潜在风险。

title: Reimagining Therapy: Consumer Perceptions of Generative <scp>AI</scp> in Mental Health
title_zh: 重塑心理治疗：消费者对生成式AI在心理健康领域的认知
